picks.reason: A personal chat produces a four-line value ledger, tying monthly fees to changed work.
Personal messaging yields a fixed five-line brief that teammates and developers can immediately use.
Pasted output becomes an evidence map that preserves useful work while exposing the riskiest gap.
A sent error becomes a precise ownership route and an actionable access request.
The personal reply separates harmless mess from recurring cost, protecting attention without selling needless cleanup.
The direct promise delays solutions until outcome, constraint, and unknown form a trustworthy question.
